─[ Claude Global Hooks Conflict ]─ ┐

name: claude-global-hooks-conflict description: Diagnose and fix duplicate hook messages from global Claude Code settings user-invocable: false allowed-tools: Bash, Read

Claude Global Hooks Conflict

Context

Claude Code loads hooks from TWO locations: (1) Project-local: .claude/settings.json and (2) Global: ~/.claude/settings.json. When both exist, hooks from BOTH locations fire, causing duplicate messages and unexpected behavior.

Snags

  • Multiple “UserPromptSubmit hook success” messages (more than one per prompt)
  • Hook output appearing multiple times
  • Hooks referencing missing files or old paths

Diagnosis

Check if global hooks exist:

# Unix/Mac
cat "$HOME/.claude/settings.json"

# Windows
cat "$USERPROFILE/.claude/settings.json"

If the file exists and contains hooks, they will fire in addition to project-local hooks.

Workaround

Clear global hooks to allow only project-local hooks:

# Unix/Mac
echo '{}' > "$HOME/.claude/settings.json"

# Windows
echo '{}' > "$USERPROFILE/.claude/settings.json"

This preserves the file (some tools expect it) but removes all hook configuration.

Prevention

  • Keep hook configuration in project .claude/settings.json only.
  • Avoid configuring global hooks unless you want them to fire for ALL projects.
  • For Lore/CHAPPiE instances: always use project-local hooks.
PgUp/PgDn: scroll b: bulk commands q: quit x: menu ← → ↑ ↓: navigate
Donate 0.24.4
